MAIN_INSIGHT: The Old Dairy is filling up nicely — 78% occupancy in the last 90 days. But at £89/night in a market where comparable Peak District cottages average £130–£160, you're subsidising every guest. High demand is your signal to raise rates, not hold them.
QUICK_WIN: Increase your base nightly rate by £25 immediately and monitor for two weeks. At 78% occupancy you can absorb a few fewer bookings and still earn significantly more per month.
FREE_TIP: Add a minimum stay of 3 nights on peak weekends (bank holidays, school breaks). It filters out low-value short stays and lifts your weekly average rate without touching your base price.
PLATFORM_2_DESC: Different audience — families, longer stays, fewer competitors. Same property, new market.
PLATFORM_2_BENCH: £6k–10k additional per year for well-listed properties
PLATFORM_3_DESC: Massive international reach. Fills gaps between Airbnb bookings, strong for shoulder seasons.
